sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.26 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: (Y.\,O.\,Hamidoune). Suppose that$A$and$B$are finite subsets of a group$G$such that$A 2$and$B 2$, and let$A _2B$denote the set of elements of$G$which can be expressed in the form$ab$for at least two different$(a,b) A B$. Is it true that$A + B - (1/2) AB - (1/2) A _2B \{2, gH \,:\, H G,\; g G,\; gH A _2B\}$? This is proved if$G$is abelian.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
